0→# My Claude Code Setup 1→ 2→> **Work in progress.** This is not meant to be a polished guide for everyone. It's mostly a summary of how I've been using Claude Code for academic work — creating lecture slides, writing R scripts, managing Beamer-to-Quarto workflows, and so on. I keep learning new things, and as I do, I keep updating these files. This is just a way for me to share what I've figured out with friends and colleagues. 3→ 4→**Live site:** [psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow](https://psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow/) 5→**Last Updated:** 2026-02-15 6→ 7→A ready-to-fork starter kit for academics using [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview) with **LaTeX/Beamer + R + Quarto**. You describe what you want; Claude plans the approach, runs specialized agents, fixes issues, verifies quality, and presents results — like a contractor who handles the entire job. Extracted from a production PhD course (6 lectures, 800+ slides). 8→ 9→--- 10→ 11→## Quick Start (5 minutes) 12→ 13→### 1. Fork & Clone 14→ 15→```bash 16→# Fork this repo on GitHub (click "Fork" on the repo page), then: 17→git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/claude-code-my-workflow.git my-project 18→cd my-project 19→``` 20→ 21→Replace `YOUR_USERNAME` with your GitHub username. 22→ 23→### 2. Start Claude Code and Paste This Prompt 24→ 25→```bash 26→claude 27→``` 28→ 29→**Using VS Code?** Open the Claude Code panel instead. Everything works the same — see the [full guide](https://psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow/workflow-guide.html#sec-setup) for details. 30→ 31→Then paste the following, filling in your project details: 32→ 33→> I am starting to work on **[PROJECT NAME]** in this repo. **[Describe your project in 2–3 sentences — what you're building, who it's for, what tools you use.]** 34→> 35→> I want our collaboration to be structured, precise, and rigorous. When creating visuals, everything must be polished and publication-ready. 36→> 37→> I've set up the Claude Code academic workflow (forked from `pedrohcgs/claude-code-my-workflow`). The configuration files are already in this repo. Please read them, understand the workflow, and then **update all configuration files to fit my project** — fill in placeholders in `CLAUDE.md`, adjust rules if needed, and propose any customizations specific to my use case. 38→> 39→> After that, use the plan-first workflow for all non-trivial tasks. Once I approve a plan, switch to contractor mode — coordinate everything autonomously and only come back to me when there's ambiguity or a decision to make. 40→> 41→> Enter plan mode and start by adapting the workflow configuration for this project. 42→ 43→**What this does:** Claude reads all the configuration files, fills in your project name, institution, and preferences, then enters contractor mode — planning, implementing, reviewing, and verifying autonomously. You approve the plan and Claude handles the rest. 44→ 45→**Prefer to configure manually?** See the [full guide](https://psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow/workflow-guide.html#sec-setup) for step-by-step manual setup instructions. 46→ 47→--- 48→ 49→## How It Works 50→ 51→### Contractor Mode 52→ 53→You describe a task. For complex or ambiguous requests, Claude first creates a requirements specification with MUST/SHOULD/MAY priorities and clarity status (CLEAR/ASSUMED/BLOCKED). You approve the spec, then Claude plans the approach, implements it, runs specialized review agents, fixes issues, re-verifies, and scores against quality gates — all autonomously. You see a summary when the work meets quality standards. Say "just do it" and it auto-commits too. 54→ 55→### Specialized Agents 56→ 57→Instead of one general-purpose reviewer, 10 focused agents each check one dimension: 58→ 59→- **proofreader** — grammar/typos 60→- **slide-auditor** — visual layout 61→- **pedagogy-reviewer** — teaching quality 62→- **r-reviewer** — R code quality 63→- **domain-reviewer** — field-specific correctness (template — customize for your field) 64→ 65→Each is better at its narrow task than a generalist would be. The `/slide-excellence` skill runs them all in parallel. 66→ 67→### Adversarial QA 68→ 69→Two agents work in opposition: the **critic** reads both Beamer and Quarto and produces harsh findings. The **fixer** implements exactly what the critic found. They loop until the critic says "APPROVED" (or 5 rounds max). This catches errors that single-pass review misses. 70→ 71→### Quality Gates 72→ 73→Every file gets a score (0–100). Scores below threshold block the action: 74→- **80** — commit threshold 75→- **90** — PR threshold 76→- **95** — excellence (aspirational) 77→ 78→### Context Survival 79→ 80→Plans, specifications, and session logs survive auto-compression and session boundaries. The PreCompact hook saves a context snapshot before Claude's auto-compression triggers, ensuring critical decisions are never lost. MEMORY.md accumulates learning across sessions, so patterns discovered in one session inform future work. 81→ 82→--- 83→ 84→## The Guide 85→ 86→For a comprehensive walkthrough, read the **[full guide](https://psantanna.com/claude-code-my-workflow/workflow-guide.html)** (or see the [source](guide/workflow-guide.qmd)). 87→ 88→It covers: 89→1. **Why This Workflow Exists** — the problem and the vision 90→2. **Getting Started** — fork, paste one prompt, and Claude sets up the rest 91→3. **The System in Action** — specialized agents, adversarial QA, quality scoring 92→4. **The Building Blocks** — CLAUDE.md, rules, skills, agents, hooks, memory 93→5. **Workflow Patterns** — lecture creation, translation, replication, multi-agent review, research exploration 94→6. **Customizing for Your Domain** — creating your own reviewers and knowledge bases 95→ 96→--- 97→ 98→## What's Included 99→ 100→
101→10 agents, 21 skills, 17 rules, 7 hooks (click to expand) 102→ 103→### Agents (`.claude/agents/`) 104→ 105→| Agent | What It Does | 106→|-------|-------------| 107→| `proofreader` | Grammar, typos, overflow, consistency review | 108→| `slide-auditor` | Visual layout audit (overflow, font consistency, spacing) | 109→| `pedagogy-reviewer` | 13-pattern pedagogical review (narrative arc, notation density, pacing) | 110→| `r-reviewer` | R code quality, reproducibility, and domain correctness | 111→| `tikz-reviewer` | Merciless TikZ diagram visual critique | 112→| `beamer-translator` | Beamer-to-Quarto translation specialist | 113→| `quarto-critic` | Adversarial QA comparing Quarto against Beamer benchmark | 114→| `quarto-fixer` | Implements fixes from the critic agent | 115→| `verifier` | End-to-end task completion verification | 116→| `domain-reviewer` | **Template** for your field-specific substance reviewer | 117→ 118→### Skills (`.claude/skills/`) 119→ 120→| Skill | What It Does | 121→|-------|-------------| 122→| `/compile-latex` | 3-pass XeLaTeX compilation with bibtex | 123→| `/deploy` | Render Quarto + sync to GitHub Pages | 124→| `/extract-tikz` | TikZ diagrams to PDF to SVG pipeline | 125→| `/proofread` | Launch proofreader on a file | 126→| `/visual-audit` | Launch slide-auditor on a file | 127→| `/pedagogy-review` | Launch pedagogy-reviewer on a file | 128→| `/review-r` | Launch R code reviewer | 129→| `/qa-quarto` | Adversarial critic-fixer loop (max 5 rounds) | 130→| `/slide-excellence` | Combined multi-agent review | 131→| `/translate-to-quarto` | Full 11-phase Beamer-to-Quarto translation | 132→| `/validate-bib` | Cross-reference citations against bibliography | 133→| `/devils-advocate` | Challenge design decisions before committing | 134→| `/create-lecture` | Full lecture creation workflow | 135→| `/commit` | Stage, commit, create PR, and merge to main | 136→| `/lit-review` | Literature search, synthesis, and gap identification | 137→| `/research-ideation` | Generate research questions and empirical strategies | 138→| `/interview-me` | Interactive interview to formalize a research idea | 139→| `/review-paper` | Manuscript review: structure, econometrics, referee objections | 140→| `/data-analysis` | End-to-end R analysis with publication-ready output | 141→| `/learn` | Extract non-obvious discoveries into persistent skills | 142→| `/context-status` | Show session health and context usage | 143→ 144→### Research Workflow 145→ 146→| Feature | What It Does | 147→|---------|-------------| 148→| Exploration folder | Structured `explorations/` sandbox with graduate/archive lifecycle | 149→| Fast-track workflow | 60/100 quality threshold for rapid prototyping | 150→| Simplified orchestrator | implement → verify → score → done (no multi-round reviews) | 151→| Enhanced session logging | Structured tables for changes, decisions, verification | 152→| Merge-only reporting | Quality reports at merge time only | 153→| Math line-length exception | Long lines acceptable for documented formulas | 154→| Workflow quick reference | One-page cheat sheet at `.claude/WORKFLOW_QUICK_REF.md` | 155→ 156→### Rules (`.claude/rules/`) 157→ 158→Rules use path-scoped loading: **always-on** rules load every session (~100 lines total); **path-scoped** rules load only when Claude works on matching files. Claude follows ~150 instructions reliably, so less is more. 159→ 160→**Always-on** (no `paths:` frontmatter — load every session): 161→ 162→| Rule | What It Enforces | 163→|------|-----------------| 164→| `plan-first-workflow` | Plan mode for non-trivial tasks + context preservation | 165→| `orchestrator-protocol` | Contractor mode: implement → verify → review → fix → score | 166→| `session-logging` | Three logging triggers: post-plan, incremental, end-of-session | 167→ 168→**Path-scoped** (load only when working on matching files): 169→ 170→| Rule | Triggers On | What It Enforces | 171→|------|------------|-----------------| 172→| `verification-protocol` | `.tex`, `.qmd`, `docs/` | Task completion checklist | 173→| `single-source-of-truth` | `Figures/`, `.tex`, `.qmd` | No content duplication; Beamer is authoritative | 174→| `quality-gates` | `.tex`, `.qmd`, `*.R` | 80/90/95 scoring + tolerance thresholds | 175→| `r-code-conventions` | `*.R` | R coding standards + math line-length exception | 176→| `tikz-visual-quality` | `.tex` | TikZ diagram visual standards | 177→| `beamer-quarto-sync` | `.tex`, `.qmd` | Auto-sync Beamer edits to Quarto | 178→| `pdf-processing` | `master_supporting_docs/` | Safe large PDF handling | 179→| `proofreading-protocol` | `.tex`, `.qmd`, `quality_reports/` | Propose-first, then apply with approval | 180→| `no-pause-beamer` | `.tex` | No overlay commands in Beamer | 181→| `replication-protocol` | `*.R` | Replicate original results before extending | 182→| `knowledge-base-template` | `.tex`, `.qmd`, `*.R` | Notation/application registry template | 183→| `orchestrator-research` | `*.R`, `explorations/` | Simple orchestrator for research (no multi-round reviews) | 184→| `exploration-folder-protocol` | `explorations/` | Structured sandbox for experimental work | 185→| `exploration-fast-track` | `explorations/` | Lightweight exploration workflow (60/100 threshold) | 186→ 187→### Templates (`templates/`) 188→ 189→| Template | What It Does | 190→|----------|-------------| 191→| `session-log.md` | Structured session logging format | 192→| `quality-report.md` | Merge-time quality report format | 193→| `exploration-readme.md` | Exploration project README template | 194→| `archive-readme.md` | Archive documentation template | 195→| `requirements-spec.md` | MUST/SHOULD/MAY requirements framework with clarity status | 196→| `constitutional-governance.md` | Template for defining non-negotiable principles vs. preferences | 197→| `skill-template.md` | Academic skill creation template with domain-specific examples | 198→ 199→
200→ 201→--- 202→ 203→## Prerequisites 204→ 205→| Tool | Required For | Install | 206→|------|-------------|---------| 207→| [Claude Code](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview) | Everything | `npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code` | 208→| XeLaTeX | LaTeX compilation | [TeX Live](https://tug.org/texlive/) or [MacTeX](https://tug.org/mactex/) | 209→| [Quarto](https://quarto.org) | Web slides | [quarto.org/docs/get-started](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) | 210→| R | Figures & analysis | [r-project.org](https://www.r-project.org/) | 211→| pdf2svg | TikZ to SVG | `brew install pdf2svg` (macOS) | 212→| [gh CLI](https://cli.github.com/) | PR workflow | `brew install gh` (macOS) | 213→ 214→Not all tools are needed — install only what your project uses. Claude Code is the only hard requirement. 215→ 216→--- 217→ 218→## Adapting for Your Field 219→ 220→1. **Fill in the knowledge base** (`.claude/rules/knowledge-base-template.md`) with your notation, applications, and design principles 221→2. **Customize the domain reviewer** (`.claude/agents/domain-reviewer.md`) with review lenses specific to your field 222→3. **Update the color palette** in your Quarto theme SCSS file — change the color variables at the top 223→4. **Add field-specific R pitfalls** to `.claude/rules/r-code-conventions.md` 224→5. **Fill in the lecture mapping** in `.claude/rules/beamer-quarto-sync.md` 225→6. **Customize the workflow quick reference** (`.claude/WORKFLOW_QUICK_REF.md`) with your non-negotiables and preferences 226→7. **Set up the exploration folder** (`explorations/`) for experimental work 227→ 228→--- 229→ 230→## Additional Resources 231→ 232→- [Claude Code Documentation](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/overview) 233→- [Writing a Good CLAUDE.md](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory) — official guidance on project memory 234→ 235→--- 236→ 237→## Origin 238→ 239→This infrastructure was extracted from **Econ 730: Causal Panel Data** at Emory University, developed by Pedro Sant'Anna using Claude Code over 6+ sessions. The course produced 6 complete PhD lecture decks with 800+ slides, interactive Quarto versions with plotly charts, and full R replication packages — all managed through this multi-agent workflow. 240→ 241→--- 242→ 243→## License 244→ 245→MIT License. Use freely for teaching, research, or any academic purpose. 246→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

0→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     1→"""
     2→Quality Scoring System for Academic Course Materials
     3→
     4→Calculates objective quality scores (0-100) based on defined rubrics.
     5→Enforces quality gates: 80 (commit), 90 (PR), 95 (excellence).
     6→
     7→Usage:
     8→    python scripts/quality_score.py Quarto/Lecture6_Topic.qmd
     9→    python scripts/quality_score.py Quarto/Lecture6_Topic.qmd --summary
    10→    python scripts/quality_score.py Quarto/*.qmd
    11→    python scripts/quality_score.py Slides/Lecture01_Topic.tex
    12→    python scripts/quality_score.py scripts/R/Lecture06_simulations.R
    13→"""
    14→
    15→import sys
    16→import argparse
    17→import subprocess
    18→from pathlib import Path
    19→from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
    20→import re
    21→import json
    22→
    23→# ==============================================================================
    24→# SCORING RUBRIC (from .claude/rules/quality-gates.md)
    25→# ==============================================================================
    26→
    27→QUARTO_RUBRIC = {
    28→    'critical': {
    29→        'compilation_failure': {'points': 100, 'auto_fail': True},
    30→        'equation_overflow': {'points': 20},
    31→        'broken_citation': {'points': 15},
    32→        'typo_in_equation': {'points': 10},
    33→        'missing_plotly_chart': {'points': 10},
    34→    },
    35→    'major': {
    36→        'text_overflow': {'points': 5},
    37→        'tikz_label_overlap': {'points': 5},
    38→        'notation_inconsistency': {'points': 3},
    39→        'missing_box_separation': {'points': 2},
    40→        'color_contrast_low': {'points': 3},
    41→    },
    42→    'minor': {
    43→        'font_size_reduction': {'points': 1},
    44→        'missing_forward_ref': {'points': 1},
    45→        'missing_framing_sentence': {'points': 1},
    46→    }
    47→}
    48→
    49→R_SCRIPT_RUBRIC = {
    50→    'critical': {
    51→        'syntax_error': {'points': 100, 'auto_fail': True},
    52→        'hardcoded_path': {'points': 20},
    53→        'missing_library': {'points': 10},
    54→    },
    55→    'major': {
    56→        'missing_set_seed': {'points': 10},
    57→        'missing_figure': {'points': 5},
    58→        'missing_rds': {'points': 5},
    59→    },
    60→    'minor': {
    61→        'style_violation': {'points': 1},
    62→        'missing_roxygen': {'points': 1},
    63→    }
    64→}
    65→
    66→BEAMER_RUBRIC = {
    67→    'critical': {
    68→        'compilation_failure': {'points': 100, 'auto_fail': True},
    69→        'undefined_citation': {'points': 15},
    70→        'overfull_hbox': {'points': 10},
    71→    },
    72→    'major': {
    73→        'text_overflow': {'points': 5},
    74→        'notation_inconsistency': {'points': 3},
    75→    },
    76→    'minor': {
    77→        'font_size_reduction': {'points': 1},
    78→    }
    79→}
    80→
    81→THRESHOLDS = {
    82→    'commit': 80,
    83→    'pr': 90,
    84→    'excellence': 95
    85→}
    86→
    87→# ==============================================================================
    88→# ISSUE DETECTION (Lightweight checks - full agents run separately)
    89→# ==============================================================================
    90→
    91→class IssueDetector:
    92→    """Detect common issues for quality scoring."""
    93→
    94→    @staticmethod
    95→    def check_quarto_compilation(filepath: Path) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
    96→        """Check if Quarto file compiles successfully."""
    97→        try:
    98→            result = subprocess.run(
    99→                ['quarto', 'render', str(filepath), '--to', 'html'],
   100→                capture_output=True,
   101→                text=True,
   102→                timeout=120,
   103→                cwd=filepath.parent
   104→            )
   105→            if result.returncode != 0:
   106→                return False, result.stderr
   107→            return True, ""
   108→        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   109→            return False, "Compilation timeout (>2min)"
   110→        except FileNotFoundError:
   111→            return False, "Quarto not installed"
   112→
   113→    @staticmethod
   114→    def check_equation_overflow(content: str) -> List[int]:
   115→        """Detect displayed equations with single lines likely to overflow.
   116→
   117→        Flags equations only when a SINGLE LINE within a math block exceeds
   118→        120 characters. Multi-line equations properly broken across lines
   119→        are not flagged even if the total block is long.
   120→
   121→        Checks:
   122→        - $$ ... $$ blocks (Quarto/LaTeX)
   123→        - \\begin{equation} ... \\end{equation} blocks
   124→        - \\begin{align} ... \\end{align} blocks
   125→        - \\begin{gather} ... \\end{gather} blocks
   126→        """
   127→        overflows = []
   128→        lines = content.split('\n')
   129→        in_math = False
   130→        math_start = 0
   131→        math_delim = None  # Track which delimiter opened the block
   132→
   133→        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   134→            stripped = line.strip()
   135→
   136→            # Check for $$ delimiter (toggle)
   137→            if '$$' in stripped and math_delim != 'env':
   138→                if not in_math:
   139→                    in_math = True
   140→                    math_start = i
   141→                    math_delim = '$$'
   142→                    # Handle single-line $$ ... $$ (both delimiters on same line)
   143→                    if stripped.count('$$') >= 2:
   144→                        inner = stripped.split('$$')[1]
   145→                        if len(inner.strip()) > 120:
   146→                            overflows.append(i)
   147→                        in_math = False
   148→                        math_delim = None
   149→                    continue
   150→                else:
   151→                    in_math = False
   152→                    math_delim = None
   153→                    continue
   154→
   155→            # Check for \begin{equation/align/gather/...}
   156→            env_begin = re.match(
   157→                r'\\begin\{(equation|align|gather|multline|eqnarray)\*?\}', stripped
   158→            )
   159→            if env_begin and not in_math:
   160→                in_math = True
   161→                math_start = i
   162→                math_delim = 'env'
   163→                continue
   164→
   165→            # Check for \end{equation/align/gather/...}
   166→            if re.match(r'\\end\{(equation|align|gather|multline|eqnarray)\*?\}', stripped):
   167→                in_math = False
   168→                math_delim = None
   169→                continue
   170→
   171→            # Inside a math block: check individual line length
   172→            if in_math:
   173→                # Strip LaTeX comments before measuring
   174→                code_part = line.split('%')[0] if '%' in line else line
   175→                if len(code_part.strip()) > 120:
   176→                    overflows.append(i)
   177→
   178→        return overflows
   179→
   180→    @staticmethod
   181→    def check_broken_citations(content: str, bib_file: Path) -> List[str]:
   182→        """Check for LaTeX citation keys not in bibliography.
   183→
   184→        Matches \\cite{}, \\citep{}, \\citet{}, \\citeauthor{}, \\citeyear{}, etc.
   185→        """
   186→        cite_pattern = r'\\cite[a-z]*\{([^}]+)\}'
   187→        cited_keys = set()
   188→        for match in re.finditer(cite_pattern, content):
   189→            keys = match.group(1).split(',')
   190→            cited_keys.update(k.strip() for k in keys)
   191→
   192→        if not bib_file.exists():
   193→            return list(cited_keys)
   194→
   195→        bib_content = bib_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   196→        bib_keys = set(re.findall(r'@\w+\{([^,]+),', bib_content))
   197→
   198→        broken = cited_keys - bib_keys
   199→        return list(broken)
   200→
   201→    @staticmethod
   202→    def check_plotly_widgets(html_file: Path, expected: int = None) -> Tuple[int, bool]:
   203→        """Check if plotly charts rendered in HTML."""
   204→        if not html_file.exists():
   205→            return 0, False
   206→
   207→        html_content = html_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   208→        actual_count = html_content.count('htmlwidget')
   209→
   210→        if expected is None:
   211→            return actual_count, True
   212→
   213→        return actual_count, (actual_count >= expected)
   214→
   215→    @staticmethod
   216→    def check_r_syntax(filepath: Path) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
   217→        """Check R script for syntax errors."""
   218→        try:
   219→            result = subprocess.run(
   220→                ['Rscript', '-e', f'parse("{filepath}")'],
   221→                capture_output=True,
   222→                text=True,
   223→                timeout=10
   224→            )
   225→            if result.returncode != 0:
   226→                return False, result.stderr
   227→            return True, ""
   228→        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   229→            return False, "Syntax check timeout"
   230→        except FileNotFoundError:
   231→            return False, "Rscript not installed"
   232→
   233→    @staticmethod
   234→    def check_hardcoded_paths(content: str) -> List[int]:
   235→        """Detect absolute paths in R scripts."""
   236→        issues = []
   237→        lines = content.split('\n')
   238→
   239→        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   240→            if re.search(r'["\'][/\\]|["\'][A-Za-z]:[/\\]', line):
   241→                if not re.search(r'http:|https:|file://|/tmp/', line):
   242→                    issues.append(i)
   243→
   244→        return issues
   245→
   246→    @staticmethod
   247→    def check_latex_syntax(content: str) -> List[Dict]:
   248→        """Check for common LaTeX syntax issues without compiling.
   249→
   250→        Looks for:
   251→        - Unmatched braces
   252→        - Unclosed environments
   253→        - Common typos in commands
   254→        """
   255→        issues = []
   256→        lines = content.split('\n')
   257→
   258→        # Track open environments
   259→        env_stack = []
   260→        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   261→            # Skip comments
   262→            stripped = line.split('%')[0] if '%' in line else line
   263→
   264→            # Check for \begin{env}
   265→            for match in re.finditer(r'\\begin\{(\w+)\}', stripped):
   266→                env_stack.append((match.group(1), i))
   267→
   268→            # Check for \end{env}
   269→            for match in re.finditer(r'\\end\{(\w+)\}', stripped):
   270→                env_name = match.group(1)
   271→                if env_stack and env_stack[-1][0] == env_name:
   272→                    env_stack.pop()
   273→                elif env_stack:
   274→                    issues.append({
   275→                        'line': i,
   276→                        'description': f'Mismatched environment: \\end{{{env_name}}} '
   277→                                       f'but expected \\end{{{env_stack[-1][0]}}} '
   278→                                       f'(opened at line {env_stack[-1][1]})',
   279→                    })
   280→                else:
   281→                    issues.append({
   282→                        'line': i,
   283→                        'description': f'\\end{{{env_name}}} without matching \\begin',
   284→                    })
   285→
   286→        # Report unclosed environments
   287→        for env_name, line_num in env_stack:
   288→            issues.append({
   289→                'line': line_num,
   290→                'description': f'Unclosed environment: \\begin{{{env_name}}} never closed',
   291→            })
   292→
   293→        return issues
   294→
   295→    @staticmethod
   296→    def check_overfull_hbox_risk(content: str) -> List[int]:
   297→        """Detect lines in LaTeX source likely to cause overfull hbox.
   298→
   299→        Checks for very long lines inside text and math environments
   300→        that are likely to overflow the slide width.
   301→        """
   302→        issues = []
   303→        lines = content.split('\n')
   304→        in_frame = False
   305→
   306→        for i, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   307→            stripped = line.split('%')[0] if '%' in line else line
   308→
   309→            # Track frame environments for context
   310→            if r'\begin{frame}' in stripped:
   311→                in_frame = True
   312→            elif r'\end{frame}' in stripped:
   313→                in_frame = False
   314→
   315→            # Flag very long content lines inside frames
   316→            # Strip leading whitespace and LaTeX commands for length check
   317→            if in_frame and len(stripped.strip()) > 120:
   318→                # Skip lines that are just comments or common long commands
   319→                if stripped.strip().startswith('%'):
   320→                    continue
   321→                # Skip includegraphics, input, and similar path-based commands
   322→                if re.match(r'\s*\\(includegraphics|input|bibliography|usepackage)', stripped):
   323→                    continue
   324→                issues.append(i)
   325→
   326→        return issues
   327→
   328→    @staticmethod
   329→    def check_quarto_citations(content: str, bib_file: Path) -> List[str]:
   330→        """Check Quarto-style citation keys against bibliography.
   331→
   332→        Supports patterns: @key, [@key], [@key1; @key2]
   333→        """
   334→        cited_keys = set()
   335→
   336→        # Pattern 1: [@key] or [@key1; @key2; ...]
   337→        bracket_pattern = r'\[([^\]]*@[^\]]+)\]'
   338→        for match in re.finditer(bracket_pattern, content):
   339→            inner = match.group(1)
   340→            # Extract individual @key references from within brackets
   341→            for key_match in re.finditer(r'@([\w:.#$%&\-+?<>~/]+)', inner):
   342→                cited_keys.add(key_match.group(1))
   343→
   344→        # Pattern 2: standalone @key (not inside brackets, not email addresses)
   345→        # Match @key that is preceded by start-of-line or whitespace or punctuation
   346→        # but NOT preceded by characters that indicate an email address
   347→        standalone_pattern = r'(?<![.\w])@([\w:.#$%&\-+?<>~/]+)'
   348→        for match in re.finditer(standalone_pattern, content):
   349→            key = match.group(1)
   350→            # Skip if it looks like a Quarto directive or special syntax
   351→            if key.startswith('{') or key in ('fig', 'tbl', 'sec', 'eq', 'lst'):
   352→                continue
   353→            cited_keys.add(key)
   354→
   355→        if not cited_keys:
   356→            return []
   357→
   358→        if not bib_file.exists():
   359→            return list(cited_keys)
   360→
   361→        bib_content = bib_file.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   362→        bib_keys = set(re.findall(r'@\w+\{([^,]+),', bib_content))
   363→
   364→        broken = cited_keys - bib_keys
   365→        return list(broken)
   366→
   367→# ==============================================================================
   368→# QUALITY SCORER
   369→# ==============================================================================
   370→
   371→class QualityScorer:
   372→    """Calculate quality scores for course materials."""
   373→
   374→    def __init__(self, filepath: Path, verbose: bool = False):
   375→        self.filepath = filepath
   376→        self.verbose = verbose
   377→        self.score = 100
   378→        self.issues = {
   379→            'critical': [],
   380→            'major': [],
   381→            'minor': []
   382→        }
   383→        self.auto_fail = False
   384→
   385→    def score_quarto(self) -> Dict:
   386→        """Score Quarto lecture slides."""
   387→        content = self.filepath.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   388→
   389→        # Check compilation
   390→        compiles, error = IssueDetector.check_quarto_compilation(self.filepath)
   391→        if not compiles:
   392→            self.auto_fail = True
   393→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   394→                'type': 'compilation_failure',
   395→                'description': 'Quarto compilation failed',
   396→                'details': error[:200],
   397→                'points': 100
   398→            })
   399→            self.score = 0
   400→            return self._generate_report()
   401→
   402→        # Check equation overflow (heuristic)
   403→        equation_overflows = IssueDetector.check_equation_overflow(content)
   404→        for line in equation_overflows:
   405→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   406→                'type': 'equation_overflow',
   407→                'description': f'Potential equation overflow at line {line}',
   408→                'details': 'Single equation line >120 chars may overflow slide',
   409→                'points': 20
   410→            })
   411→            self.score -= 20
   412→
   413→        # Check broken citations (LaTeX-style \cite patterns)
   414→        bib_file = self.filepath.parent.parent / 'Bibliography_base.bib'
   415→        broken_citations = IssueDetector.check_broken_citations(content, bib_file)
   416→
   417→        # Also check Quarto-style @key citations
   418→        quarto_broken = IssueDetector.check_quarto_citations(content, bib_file)
   419→        # Merge both sets, avoiding duplicates
   420→        all_broken = set(broken_citations) | set(quarto_broken)
   421→        for key in all_broken:
   422→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   423→                'type': 'broken_citation',
   424→                'description': f'Citation key not in bibliography: {key}',
   425→                'details': 'Add to Bibliography_base.bib or fix key',
   426→                'points': 15
   427→            })
   428→            self.score -= 15
   429→
   430→        # Check plotly widgets (if HTML exists)
   431→        html_file = self.filepath.parent.parent / 'docs' / 'slides' / self.filepath.with_suffix('.html').name
   432→        if html_file.exists():
   433→            widget_count, _ = IssueDetector.check_plotly_widgets(html_file)
   434→            expected_plotly = content.count('plotly::plot_ly')
   435→            if expected_plotly > 0 and widget_count < expected_plotly:
   436→                missing = expected_plotly - widget_count
   437→                self.issues['critical'].append({
   438→                    'type': 'missing_plotly_chart',
   439→                    'description': f'{missing} plotly chart(s) failed to render',
   440→                    'details': f'Expected {expected_plotly}, found {widget_count}',
   441→                    'points': 10 * missing
   442→                })
   443→                self.score -= 10 * missing
   444→
   445→        self.score = max(0, self.score)
   446→        return self._generate_report()
   447→
   448→    def score_r_script(self) -> Dict:
   449→        """Score R script quality."""
   450→        content = self.filepath.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   451→
   452→        # Check syntax
   453→        is_valid, error = IssueDetector.check_r_syntax(self.filepath)
   454→        if not is_valid:
   455→            self.auto_fail = True
   456→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   457→                'type': 'syntax_error',
   458→                'description': 'R syntax error',
   459→                'details': error[:200],
   460→                'points': 100
   461→            })
   462→            self.score = 0
   463→            return self._generate_report()
   464→
   465→        # Check hardcoded paths
   466→        path_issues = IssueDetector.check_hardcoded_paths(content)
   467→        for line in path_issues:
   468→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   469→                'type': 'hardcoded_path',
   470→                'description': f'Hardcoded absolute path at line {line}',
   471→                'details': 'Use relative paths or here::here()',
   472→                'points': 20
   473→            })
   474→            self.score -= 20
   475→
   476→        # Check for set.seed() if randomness detected
   477→        has_random = any(fn in content for fn in ['rnorm', 'runif', 'sample', 'rbinom', 'rnbinom'])
   478→        has_seed = 'set.seed' in content
   479→        if has_random and not has_seed:
   480→            self.issues['major'].append({
   481→                'type': 'missing_set_seed',
   482→                'description': 'Missing set.seed() for reproducibility',
   483→                'details': 'Add set.seed(YYYYMMDD) after library() calls',
   484→                'points': 10
   485→            })
   486→            self.score -= 10
   487→
   488→        self.score = max(0, self.score)
   489→        return self._generate_report()
   490→
   491→    def score_beamer(self) -> Dict:
   492→        """Score Beamer/LaTeX lecture slides."""
   493→        content = self.filepath.read_text(encoding='utf-8')
   494→
   495→        # Check for LaTeX syntax issues (without compiling)
   496→        syntax_issues = IssueDetector.check_latex_syntax(content)
   497→        if syntax_issues:
   498→            # Mismatched environments are treated as compilation risk
   499→            for issue in syntax_issues:
   500→                self.issues['critical'].append({
   501→                    'type': 'compilation_failure',
   502→                    'description': f'LaTeX syntax issue at line {issue["line"]}',
   503→                    'details': issue['description'],
   504→                    'points': 100
   505→                })
   506→            self.auto_fail = True
   507→            self.score = 0
   508→            return self._generate_report()
   509→
   510→        # Check for undefined/broken citations (\cite, \citep, \citet patterns)
   511→        bib_file = self.filepath.parent.parent / 'Bibliography_base.bib'
   512→        if not bib_file.exists():
   513→            # Also check same directory
   514→            bib_file = self.filepath.parent / 'Bibliography_base.bib'
   515→        broken_citations = IssueDetector.check_broken_citations(content, bib_file)
   516→        for key in broken_citations:
   517→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   518→                'type': 'undefined_citation',
   519→                'description': f'Citation key not in bibliography: {key}',
   520→                'details': 'Add to Bibliography_base.bib or fix key',
   521→                'points': 15
   522→            })
   523→            self.score -= 15
   524→
   525→        # Check for lines likely to cause overfull hbox
   526→        overfull_lines = IssueDetector.check_overfull_hbox_risk(content)
   527→        for line in overfull_lines:
   528→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   529→                'type': 'overfull_hbox',
   530→                'description': f'Potential overfull hbox at line {line}',
   531→                'details': 'Line >120 chars inside frame may overflow slide width',
   532→                'points': 10
   533→            })
   534→            self.score -= 10
   535→
   536→        # Check equation overflow (same heuristic as Quarto)
   537→        equation_overflows = IssueDetector.check_equation_overflow(content)
   538→        for line_num in equation_overflows:
   539→            self.issues['critical'].append({
   540→                'type': 'overfull_hbox',
   541→                'description': f'Potential equation overflow at line {line_num}',
   542→                'details': 'Single equation line >120 chars likely to overflow',
   543→                'points': 10
   544→            })
   545→            self.score -= 10
   546→
   547→        self.score = max(0, self.score)
   548→        return self._generate_report()
   549→
   550→    def _generate_report(self) -> Dict:
   551→        """Generate quality score report."""
   552→        if self.auto_fail:
   553→            status = 'FAIL'
   554→            threshold = 'None (auto-fail)'
   555→        elif self.score >= THRESHOLDS['excellence']:
   556→            status = 'EXCELLENCE'
   557→            threshold = 'excellence'
   558→        elif self.score >= THRESHOLDS['pr']:
   559→            status = 'PR_READY'
   560→            threshold = 'pr'
   561→        elif self.score >= THRESHOLDS['commit']:
   562→            status = 'COMMIT_READY'
   563→            threshold = 'commit'
   564→        else:
   565→            status = 'BLOCKED'
   566→            threshold = 'None (below commit)'
   567→
   568→        critical_count = len(self.issues['critical'])
   569→        major_count = len(self.issues['major'])
   570→        minor_count = len(self.issues['minor'])
   571→        total_count = critical_count + major_count + minor_count
   572→
   573→        return {
   574→            'filepath': str(self.filepath),
   575→            'score': self.score,
   576→            'status': status,
   577→            'threshold': threshold,
   578→            'auto_fail': self.auto_fail,
   579→            'issues': {
   580→                'critical': self.issues['critical'],
   581→                'major': self.issues['major'],
   582→                'minor': self.issues['minor'],
   583→                'counts': {
   584→                    'critical': critical_count,
   585→                    'major': major_count,
   586→                    'minor': minor_count,
   587→                    'total': total_count
   588→                }
   589→            },
   590→            'thresholds': THRESHOLDS
   591→        }
   592→
   593→    def print_report(self, summary_only: bool = False) -> None:
   594→        """Print formatted quality report."""
   595→        report = self._generate_report()
   596→
   597→        print(f"\n# Quality Score: {self.filepath.name}\n")
   598→
   599→        status_emoji = {
   600→            'EXCELLENCE': '[EXCELLENCE]',
   601→            'PR_READY': '[PASS]',
   602→            'COMMIT_READY': '[PASS]',
   603→            'BLOCKED': '[BLOCKED]',
   604→            'FAIL': '[FAIL]'
   605→        }
   606→
   607→        print(f"## Overall Score: {report['score']}/100 {status_emoji.get(report['status'], '')}")
   608→
   609→        if report['status'] == 'BLOCKED':
   610→            print(f"\n**Status:** BLOCKED - Cannot commit (score < {THRESHOLDS['commit']})")
   611→        elif report['status'] == 'COMMIT_READY':
   612→            print(f"\n**Status:** Ready for commit (score >= {THRESHOLDS['commit']})")
   613→            gap_to_pr = THRESHOLDS['pr'] - report['score']
   614→            print(f"**Next milestone:** PR threshold ({THRESHOLDS['pr']}+)")
   615→            print(f"**Gap analysis:** Need +{gap_to_pr} points to reach PR quality")
   616→        elif report['status'] == 'PR_READY':
   617→            print(f"\n**Status:** Ready for PR (score >= {THRESHOLDS['pr']})")
   618→            gap_to_excellence = THRESHOLDS['excellence'] - report['score']
   619→            if gap_to_excellence > 0:
   620→                print(f"**Next milestone:** Excellence ({THRESHOLDS['excellence']})")
   621→                print(f"**Gap analysis:** +{gap_to_excellence} points to excellence")
   622→        elif report['status'] == 'EXCELLENCE':
   623→            print(f"\n**Status:** Excellence achieved! (score >= {THRESHOLDS['excellence']})")
   624→        elif report['status'] == 'FAIL':
   625→            print(f"\n**Status:** Auto-fail (compilation/syntax error)")
   626→
   627→        if summary_only:
   628→            print(f"\n**Total issues:** {report['issues']['counts']['total']} "
   629→                  f"({report['issues']['counts']['critical']} critical, "
   630→                  f"{report['issues']['counts']['major']} major, "
   631→                  f"{report['issues']['counts']['minor']} minor)")
   632→            return
   633→
   634→        # Detailed issues
   635→        print(f"\n## Critical Issues (MUST FIX): {report['issues']['counts']['critical']}")
   636→        if report['issues']['counts']['critical'] == 0:
   637→            print("No critical issues - safe to commit\n")
   638→        else:
   639→            for i, issue in enumerate(report['issues']['critical'], 1):
   640→                print(f"{i}. **{issue['description']}** (-{issue['points']} points)")
   641→                print(f"   - {issue['details']}\n")
   642→
   643→        if report['issues']['counts']['major'] > 0:
   644→            print(f"## Major Issues (SHOULD FIX): {report['issues']['counts']['major']}")
   645→            for i, issue in enumerate(report['issues']['major'], 1):
   646→                print(f"{i}. **{issue['description']}** (-{issue['points']} points)")
   647→                print(f"   - {issue['details']}\n")
   648→
   649→        if report['issues']['counts']['minor'] > 0 and self.verbose:
   650→            print(f"## Minor Issues (NICE-TO-HAVE): {report['issues']['counts']['minor']}")
   651→            for i, issue in enumerate(report['issues']['minor'], 1):
   652→                print(f"{i}. {issue['description']} (-{issue['points']} points)\n")
   653→
   654→        # Recommendations
   655→        if report['status'] == 'BLOCKED':
   656→            print("## Recommended Actions")
   657→            print("1. Fix all critical issues above")
   658→            print(f"2. Re-run quality score (target: >={THRESHOLDS['commit']})")
   659→            print("3. Commit after reaching threshold\n")
   660→        elif report['status'] == 'COMMIT_READY' and report['score'] < THRESHOLDS['pr']:
   661→            print("## Recommended Actions to Reach PR Threshold")
   662→            points_needed = THRESHOLDS['pr'] - report['score']
   663→            print(f"Need +{points_needed} points to reach {THRESHOLDS['pr']}/100")
   664→            if report['issues']['counts']['major'] > 0:
   665→                print("Fix major issues listed above to improve score")
   666→            print(f"\n**Estimated time:** 10-20 minutes\n")
   667→
   668→# ==============================================================================
   669→# CLI INTERFACE
   670→# ==============================================================================
   671→
   672→def main():
   673→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
   674→        description='Calculate quality scores for course materials',
   675→        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,
   676→        epilog="""
   677→Examples:
   678→  # Score a single Quarto file
   679→  python scripts/quality_score.py Quarto/Lecture6_Topic.qmd
   680→
   681→  # Score multiple files
   682→  python scripts/quality_score.py Quarto/*.qmd
   683→
   684→  # Score a Beamer/LaTeX file
   685→  python scripts/quality_score.py Slides/Lecture01_Topic.tex
   686→
   687→  # Score an R script
   688→  python scripts/quality_score.py scripts/R/Lecture06_simulations.R
   689→
   690→  # Summary only (no detailed issues)
   691→  python scripts/quality_score.py Quarto/Lecture6.qmd --summary
   692→
   693→  # Verbose output (include minor issues)
   694→  python scripts/quality_score.py Quarto/Lecture6.qmd --verbose
   695→
   696→Quality Thresholds:
   697→  80/100 = Commit threshold (blocks if below)
   698→  90/100 = PR threshold (warning if below)
   699→  95/100 = Excellence (aspirational)
   700→
   701→Exit Codes:
   702→  0 = Score >= 80 (commit allowed)
   703→  1 = Score < 80 (commit blocked)
   704→  2 = Auto-fail (compilation/syntax error)
   705→        """
   706→    )
   707→
   708→    parser.add_argument('filepaths', type=Path, nargs='+', help='Path(s) to file(s) to score')
   709→    parser.add_argument('--summary', action='store_true', help='Show summary only')
   710→    parser.add_argument('--verbose', action='store_true', help='Show all issues including minor')
   711→    parser.add_argument('--json', action='store_true', help='Output as JSON')
   712→
   713→    args = parser.parse_args()
   714→
   715→    results = []
   716→    exit_code = 0
   717→
   718→    for filepath in args.filepaths:
   719→        if not filepath.exists():
   720→            print(f"Error: File not found: {filepath}")
   721→            exit_code = 1
   722→            continue
   723→
   724→        try:
   725→            scorer = QualityScorer(filepath, verbose=args.verbose)
   726→
   727→            if filepath.suffix == '.qmd':
   728→                report = scorer.score_quarto()
   729→            elif filepath.suffix == '.R':
   730→                report = scorer.score_r_script()
   731→            elif filepath.suffix == '.tex':
   732→                report = scorer.score_beamer()
   733→            else:
   734→                print(f"Error: Unsupported file type: {filepath.suffix}")
   735→                continue
   736→
   737→            results.append(report)
   738→
   739→            if not args.json:
   740→                scorer.print_report(summary_only=args.summary)
   741→
   742→            if report['auto_fail']:
   743→                exit_code = max(exit_code, 2)
   744→            elif report['score'] < THRESHOLDS['commit']:
   745→                exit_code = max(exit_code, 1)
   746→
   747→        except Exception as e:
   748→            print(f"Error scoring {filepath}: {e}")
   749→            import traceback
   750→            traceback.print_exc()
   751→            exit_code = 1
   752→
   753→    if args.json:
   754→        print(json.dumps(results, indent=2))
   755→
   756→    sys.exit(exit_code)
   757→
   758→if __name__ == '__main__':
   759→    main()
   760→

I searched the requested areas for references to scheduling tools, daily summaries, email integration, or automation. Here’s what I found:

1. `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0f1dc8f/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/README.md` – Project-level guide and workflow overview; no mentions of daily digests, scheduling, or automated email flows, but the file describes the general academic automation framework, central agents/skills, and deployment steps.

2. `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0f1dc8f/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/scripts/quality_score.py` – Python script that enforces quality gates (80/90/95) for Quarto/Beamer/R files. It does not mention scheduling, digests, or email integrations; it’s focused solely on scoring and reporting quality issues for slides and scripts.

3. `/Users/my/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0f1dc8f/.Trash/my-project 19.06.43/quality_reports/` – Contains session logs and plans, but grep searches for “daily,” “digest,” “schedule,” “automation,” or “email” returned no matches, so there’s no explicit daily digest or email automation documented there.

No explicit scheduling tools, daily summary generators, or email automation examples could be found in the inspected README, scripts, or quality reports.
